mechanism: Azure Policy denies TLS versions below 1.2 at the management group level. Exceptions require a policy exemption.
why: Without explicit denial, traffic silently downgrades to protocols with known cryptographic weaknesses. Attackers can force a downgrade without the operator being aware.

related.note: Without TLS enforcement, any legitimately public endpoint (via exemption) has uncontrolled transport.
Security deny policies are never in scope for Defender exemptions.
Must use a Deny-prefixed assignment for TLS compliance.
Self-service exemptions make hard denies viable.
A compliant default does not substitute for a Deny gate.

violations: Storage account with minimumTlsVersion below TLS1_2.
App Service with minimum TLS version below 1.2.
